title: Grafana Dashboard Load Time P95 Trending
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:log` HTTP access lines with `path=/api/dashboards/*` and `duration_ms` or parseable latency
app: Grafana file or Loki shipping, Splunk Connect for Grafana, or reverse-proxy JSON logs
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log"
| search "/api/dashboards" OR "/d/"
| eval ms=coalesce(duration_ms, request_duration_ms, response_time_ms)
| where isnotnull(ms)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats perc95(ms) as p95_load_ms, count as hits by _time, host
| eventstats median(p95_load_ms) as baseline by host
| where p95_load_ms > baseline * 1.4 AND hits > 30
| sort -p95_load_ms
description: Slow dashboard loads frustrate incident responders and hide behind average metrics when a few heavy clients dominate. Tracking p95 per Grafana instance catches database pressure, plugin overhead, and auth latency regressions early.
value: Keeps operational and executive dashboards usable during incidents when seconds matter.
implementation: Prefer structured JSON access logs; if only text, `rex` latency from nginx-style fields. Exclude asset paths (`public/build`). Split by `org_id` if present. Correlate with Postgres slow-query logs.
visualization: Line chart (p95 ms), histogram of latency, top dashboards by total time (sum of ms).
grandmaExplanation: We measure how long it takes people to open dashboards. If that suddenly gets much slower, we flag it so troubleshooting screens stay fast when teams are already stressed.
